description: Wire the already-ported AST/sema checker into the live path, prove parity, delete internal/check, then prune internal/analyze and the text/scanner-based half of internal/scan — removing the last lexer crutch before self-host.

userStories.title: Add foreign enrichment to sema.Info
userStories.description: As the checker, I need out-of-package struct fields and method signatures resolved into sema.Info so that AST-based checks can reason about imported types without analyze.Tables.
acceptanceCriteria: sema.Info exposes a ForeignMethods map (pkg.Type.Method -> FuncSig) and imported struct fields are populated into the same Structs map sema already exposes
A sema.EnrichForeign(info, imports []*ast.ImportSpec, dir string, resolve DirResolver) []error function exists and reads imported package declarations via the Go toolchain, driven by ast.File.Imports
sema.EnrichForeign and its helpers contain no call to scan.Lex or analyze.ParseImports (imports come from the AST, not re-lexing)
DirResolver and DefaultResolver are available from internal/sema
A test enriches a multi-file fixture with an imported struct and method and asserts the resolved ForeignMethods + Structs entries match what analyze.EnrichForeign produces for the same input
userStories.notes: Decision: ForeignMethods lives directly on sema.Info to mirror analyze.Tables. This unit is shared with US-009 (backend) — build it once. Reuse the Go-toolchain reader logic from internal/analyze/foreign.go.

userStories.title: Add sema package-check driver
userStories.description: As cmd/goal, I need a package-level sema check entry point so that multi-file packages produce per-file diagnostics from the AST checker.
acceptanceCriteria: A sema package driver (e.g. AnalyzePackageInDir(srcs []string, dir string) ([][]Diagnostic, error)) parses each file, calls ResolvePackage, runs EnrichForeign, then runs sema.Check per file
The driver returns one []Diagnostic per input file in input order
A test over a multi-file package fixture returns the expected diagnostics, including at least one finding that depends on foreign enrichment from US-001
userStories.notes: Mirrors the shape of the legacy check.AnalyzePackageInDir so US-004 is a near drop-in swap. sema.Diagnostic.Pos is a token.Pos already carrying Line/Col, so no OffsetToPosition mapping is needed downstream.

userStories.title: Add differential checker parity gate
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a test proving the sema checker and legacy checker agree on the corpus so that deleting internal/check is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: A test runs both the sema checker and the legacy internal/check over every case under testdata/check/** and compares findings by (file, line, feature, code, severity)
The test passes only when findings are identical, except for divergences explicitly recorded in DECISIONS.md

Any divergence where the AST checker fires and the legacy checker deferred is recorded in DECISIONS.md as a documented improvement, and the corresponding // want markers are updated to the sema behavior
userStories.notes: Decision: accept-and-document AST/legacy divergences rather than forcing byte-for-byte legacy match, since internal/check is being deleted. The corpus already has both adapters (corpus/check_runner.go and corpus/sema_checker.go) to build this on.

userStories.title: Rewire cmd/goal check onto sema
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need `goal check` to run the AST checker so that the legacy lexical checker is no longer on the live path.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/goal/main.go checkPackage uses the US-002 sema driver and no longer imports internal/check
The typecheck depth stage and the lexical/depth dedup are preserved (same suppress-by-(basename,line,feature) behavior)
An end-to-end test asserts `goal check` output over the corpus check cases is unchanged from before the rewire
userStories.notes: check.OffsetToPosition usage is dropped; sema diagnostics carry Line/Col directly. checkDiag and render() stay.

userStories.title: Delete internal/check package
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the legacy checker removed so that nothing live depends on it and the codebase has one checker.
acceptanceCriteria: The internal/check directory is removed
grep for an import of internal/check returns no matches outside attic/ and features/_cut/
corpus/check_runner.go and its tests are repointed to the sema checker and the legacy CheckerFunc adapter is removed
userStories.notes: After this story the only remaining analyze/scan consumers are typecheck, fix (survivor utils only), project, pipeline/sourcemap, backend/package, and lsp (resolver type).

userStories.title: Relocate scan/analyze pure survivors
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the lexer-independent helpers moved out of scan/analyze so that the scanner and analyze can later be deleted without losing pure utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: analyze.ZeroLit is relocated out of internal/analyze and all callers (fix, typecheck) import it from the new home
The pure scan utilities (Replacement, Splice, BaseType, IsIdent, IsLineStart, NextNewline, LeadIdent, SplitAssign, IsStmtKeyword) are moved into internal/textedit and all callers updated
internal/textedit and the new ZeroLit home do not import text/scanner
No behavior change: existing fix and typecheck tests pass against the relocated helpers
userStories.notes: Decision: survivors move to a new internal/textedit package (honest name) rather than a slimmed internal/scan that no longer scans. ZeroLit can live in internal/textedit or alongside sema; keep it with the other type helpers.

userStories.title: Migrate typecheck off analyze and scan
userStories.description: As the depth checker, I need to build my facts from sema.Info so that internal/typecheck no longer depends on analyze.Tables or the token scanner.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/typecheck imports neither internal/analyze nor the scan lexer (no scan.Lex/Token/Match*/ScanFuncs)
typecheck builds its function/signature facts from sema.ResolvePackage / sema.Info and the relocated ZeroLit
All existing depth-check tests pass
userStories.notes: typecheck.go uses analyze.BuildPackage + Tables; mustuse.go uses analyze.ModeResult; implements.go uses scan.Lex to find `type X struct`. Replace each with sema.Info / AST walks.

userStories.title: Migrate project and sourcemap off scan
userStories.description: As the build/source-map layer, I need package-name detection and decl-span mapping built from the AST so that internal/project and internal/pipeline/sourcemap no longer use the token scanner.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/project and internal/pipeline/sourcemap import neither the scan lexer (scan.Lex/Token/Match*) nor internal/analyze
Package-name detection and the source map are derived from parser.ParseFile / AST node offsets (FuncDecl.Pos()/End() etc.)
Existing project and source-map tests pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 for any retained pure helpers. The sourcemap can key declaration spans off AST node offsets the way internal/fix already does.

userStories.title: Migrate backend package onto sema enrichment
userStories.description: As the backend, I need foreign type facts from sema so that internal/backend/package.go no longer depends on analyze for lowering.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/backend imports no internal/analyze symbols
backend/package.go uses sema.ResolvePackage and sema.EnrichForeign (from US-001) for cross-file and foreign facts
The corpus behavioral conformance tier passes on the new backend path
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (sema.EnrichForeign) and US-006. backend/package.go currently calls analyze.BuildPackage + analyze.EnrichForeign at lines ~113-114 while already using sema.ResolvePackage elsewhere.

userStories.title: Delete analyze and the scanner
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need internal/analyze and the text/scanner-based lexer removed so that the last lexer crutch is gone before self-host.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/analyze is removed and grep for an import of it returns no matches outside attic/ and features/_cut/

The scan lexer symbols (Lex, Token, all Match*, ScanFuncs, Func, CalleeKey, FirstBodyBrace, ParamsClose, TopLevelComma, MatchQualifier, MatchBodyBrace, IsBareQuestionStmt) are removed from the live tree
grep for text/scanner returns matches only under attic/ and features/_cut/
userStories.notes: Final deletion. Depends on US-007, US-008, US-009 having removed every live consumer. The pure utilities relocated in US-006 survive in internal/textedit.
